summary: Register the non-destructive succession notice on the k=5 S16 certificate, preserving its exact specialization authority while routing its historical R11 forecast to the completed all-k weighted theorem.

artifacts.title: k=5 Axial Norm Galois Group — Certificate Report
artifacts.summary: Exact specialization certificate proving the generic k=5 base group S_16, now carrying a succession notice that the all-k weighted theorem supersedes its historical R11 forecast while the k=5 decorated Kummer closure remains open.

claims.statement: The generic five-charge axial mass norm has Galois group S_16; the report's former R11 forecast is superseded by the all-k weighted theorem, while its k=5 decorated-closure guardrail remains current.
hypotheses: Use the source document's stated genericity hypotheses and exact specialization certificate.
claims.scope: Five-charge base monodromy and its succession status.

declared_impacts.note: The dated k=5 certificate now routes current R11 authority to the weighted all-k successor.

llm.one_sentence_purpose: Preserve the exact k=5 S16 certificate while distinguishing its historical R11 forecast from current all-k proof authority.
retrieval_questions: What proves the k=5 base Galois group, and which later theorem generalizes it?

declared_impacts.note: No graph claim changes: IV5 is already resolved; this artifact-only cleanup aligns a dated supporting certificate.
